title: County and Municipal Budgets

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: County and Municipal Budgets
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All county and municipal budgets from year 2012-2013 to 2025-2026 for each county and municipal government as listed on the State Auditor’s website at https://www.wvsao.gov/LocalGovernment/Default. I’m requesting this data because there is not a bulk download button on the website, and the process of gathering and sending the data to me should be more simple for you than for me to individually download or scrape your website. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. On the State Auditor’s website, these files are provided as PDFs. However, I suspect that the raw data is stored in a tabular data format like spreadsheets or a relational database. If this the case, please note that I am requesting the tabular or native digital format and not the PDF reports. If there you are not in possession of any responsive records other than the PDFs, then I am requesting the PDFs themselves. 

If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Records Relating to the Investigation into Online Radicalization in the 2022 Buffalo Shooting

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Records Relating to the Investigation into Online Radicalization in the 2022 Buffalo Shooting
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to the New York Attorney General's investigation into how Payton Gendron used online platforms to plan and publicize the May 14, 2022, mass shooting at Tops Friendly Markets in Buffalo, New York. This includes but is not limited to:

- The full investigative report or any summary reports produced by the AG's office reviewing Gendron's online activity and radicalization
- Any communications between the AG's office and social media platforms (including but not limited to Twitch, Discord, and Reddit) in connection with this investigation
- Any recommendations made by the AG's office to social media platforms or state legislators arising from this investigation

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

title: ILJAOC (CA) - PSDEX/AVCC Contributions and Data Sharing

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: ILJAOC (CA) - PSDEX/AVCC Contributions and Data Sharing
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Foundational Documents: The Accurint Virtual Crime Center 'AVCC XML Addendum' or 'PSDEX Addendum' executed by ILJAOC, including all sub-agency addendums executed by member agencies.

2) Who Contributes: Any documentation, configurations, or records that indicate whether ILJAOC members contribute data to PSDEX/AVCC (Public Safety Data Exchange / Accurint Virtual Crime Center).

3) Who Can Access Agency Data: Any documentation, configurations, or records that indicate what agencies may access contributed data. Specifically, we seek to understand whether ILJAOC members who contribute data to PSDEX/AVCC restrict data access to only ILJAOC members, or if agency data flows into the national PSDEX database available to other AVCC customers.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: SOPs, data requirements, and review templates for TSCA biotech environmental-release submissions

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: SOPs, data requirements, and review templates for TSCA biotech environmental-release submissions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), desk manuals, review handbooks, or equivalent internal guidance documents used by EPA staff to review TSCA Section 5 submissions for microorganisms intended for environmental release or field testing — including TERAs, MCANs, and Tier I/II exemption notices.

Internal checklists, data requirements tables, or templates identifying the categories of information EPA requests or expects from submitters in connection with environmental-release submissions.

Risk assessment frameworks, templates, or guidance documents used to evaluate environmental-release submissions, including any documents describing how human health, ecological, and environmental risks are characterized for engineered microorganisms.

Templates used to draft consent orders, Significant New Use Rules (SNURs), or "not unreasonable risk" determinations for biotech submissions involving environmental release.
Internal training materials prepared for new reviewers in the Biotechnology Program.

I am not requesting case-specific risk assessments or submitter-specific records in this request — only the generic procedural, methodological, and training documents.
Date range: January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
Format: Electronic copies in original format (PDF, Word, Excel). Please provide records on a rolling basis as they become available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

title: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list, index, log, tracking spreadsheet, or equivalent record identifying all of the following submissions received by EPA where the submission concerns environmental release, field testing, or any intended use of the microorganism outside of contained structures:

TSCA Experimental Release Applications (TERAs)
Microbial Commercial Activity Notices (MCANs)
Tier I exemption notices
Tier II exemption notices

For each submission, I request the following non-CBI fields to the extent maintained:

Case number or docket identifier
Submission title or short description
Date of receipt
Submitter name (where not designated as CBI)
Type of submission (TERA, MCAN, Tier I, or Tier II)
Status or disposition (e.g., under review, withdrawn, dropped from review, completed, expired)
Date of final EPA action, if any
Intended use category or organism type, where captured in tracking records

I am specifically excluding submissions limited to contained use (e.g., fermentor-based industrial production with no environmental release component).
Date range: October 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
If no single tracking record exists, please provide the closest equivalent (e.g., entries exported from the Biotechnology Program's case management system).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

title: Records schedule and file plan for the OPPT Biotechnology Program

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records schedule and file plan for the OPPT Biotechnology Program
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

...specifically pertaining to the Biotechnology Program within the New Chemicals Division (or successor office responsible for microorganism submissions under TSCA Section 5):
1. The current records control schedule(s), records disposition schedule(s), and file plan(s) governing records created or maintained by the OPPT Biotechnology Program, including any schedules approved by the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA).
2. Any internal guidance, indexes, or documents that identify the categories of records the Biotechnology Program creates or maintains in connection with submissions for microorganisms under TSCA Section 5 — including but not limited to TSCA Experimental Release Applications (TERAs), Microbial Commercial Activity Notices (MCANs), Tier I exemption notices, and Tier II exemption notices.
3. Any records retention or disposal forms (e.g., SF-115 or equivalent) submitted to NARA in the last five years that cover Biotechnology Program records.

Date range: I am requesting current/active schedules and any versions in effect during the past five years.
Format: Electronic copies in their original format (PDF, Word, Excel) are preferred. Please provide records on a rolling basis as they become available rather than waiting for the entire request to be processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

title: Correspondence Between DOS and ALPR Vendors

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Correspondence Between DOS and ALPR Vendors
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am looking for a few pieces of information related to:
CR26-0246, a contract between the city of Denver and Axon Enterprise Inc. allowing for the purchase and install of multiple ALPR Units (https://denver.legistar.com/LegislationDetail.aspx?ID=7938541&GUID=093F64CB-3951-4C69-9B4B-14B57D302CA6&Options=&Search=)
Denver Police Department had a pilot program and then a contract with Flock Safety that the police found very useful. They then cancelled that contract and entered into a new contract with Axon Enterprise Inc. I'm hoping to find out what about the old system or the old vendor police and/or city leadership didn't like, as well as what they do like about this new system. I’d also like to find out what the police hoped to get out of the old system, how they talked about it internally, and what they are hoping to get out of the new system.

Please provide the following: 

1. The planned locations, or current locations of the ALPR Units from contract POLIC-202683606

2. Contract POLIC-202683606

3. Correspondence (emails, memos, instant messages, etc.) sent internally or DPD/DOS employee to Vendors of ALPR’s.
To reduce the burden and scope of this request, please limit correspondence to items dated between Jan 1, 2022 to the time this request is processed.
To further reduce the burden and scope of the request, limit correspondence with ALPR vendors to employees or parties representing Flock Safety and Axon Enterprise, Inc. (for emails: addresses ending in @flocksafety.com; @axon.com)
To further reduce the burden and scope of the request, please return correspondence with subject line or contents including key phrases such as:
Flock
Flock Safety
25-0413
ALPR
LPR
LPR's
Axon ALPR
Axon LPR
CR26-0246
CR 26-0246
CR 26 0246
26-0246
POLIC-202683606
202683606
Surveillance
Surveillance Task Force

Finally, to further limit the burden and scope of this request, limit correspondence to items where the following parties are either a sender or recipient:
Ron Thomas, Chief of Police
Al Gardner, Deputy Mayor
Emily Lauck, Policy Director
Commander Cliff Barnes, Director of DPD Cyber Bureau
Jacob Hererra
Todd Ericson

For email and other applicable mediums of correspondence, please include attachments.

I appreciate the possibility that even after the attempted limiting of scope and burden, there may still be a considerable amount of items that fit this request. Please let me know if this is the case and I will do my best to further refine the scope of the search.

Thank you for your help,
William Beckett
Resident of the Baker Neighborhood, Denver

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Beckett

title: May 14th Budget Meeting Incident

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: May 14th Budget Meeting Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request copies of all public records in the custody of the Rocky Mount Police Department related to an incident that occurred on or about May 14, 2026, at or near Rocky Mount City Hall, during or following a Rocky Mount City Council meeting.

Specifically, I request:

1. All incident reports, police reports, arrest records, and associated documentation related to the altercation on May 14, 2026, at or near City Hall;
2. All body-worn camera footage, surveillance footage, or other video or audio recordings capturing or related to the incident;
3. All dispatch records, CAD logs, and communications related to any calls for service at or near City Hall on May 14, 2026;
4. All security plans, threat assessments, or operational plans for the Rocky Mount City Council meeting held on May 14, 2026; and
5. Any after-action reports, policy reviews, or documentation reflecting security changes implemented or proposed following the incident.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon, and produce all non-exempt portions.

I request that records be provided in electronic format where available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

Peter Herring

title: Plymouth Community Preservation Committee / A.D. Makepeace December 7, 2023 record packet

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Plymouth Community Preservation Committee / A.D. Makepeace December 7, 2023 record packet
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request records from the Plymouth Selectmen's Office and/or Community Preservation Committee concerning the Plymouth Community Preservation Committee meeting held on or about December 7, 2023 and any grant, recommendation, vote, application, or agenda item involving A.D. Makepeace Company, Redbrook, Makepeace Farms, Federal Furnace Cranberry, Garden Road, or any Makepeace-affiliated entity.

Please produce:

1. Final and draft minutes for the December 7, 2023 CPC meeting.
2. Complete audio and/or video recording of the meeting.
3. Agenda, agenda packet, staff memorandum, member packet, and all attachments distributed before or during the meeting.
4. All applications, supporting materials, maps, budgets, scoring sheets, evaluation worksheets, recommendation memoranda, and vote records for any A.D. Makepeace / Makepeace-affiliated request considered at or around that meeting.
5. Roll-call votes, recusals, conflict disclosures, abstentions, or correspondence concerning the Makepeace item.
6. Communications from October 1, 2023 through February 29, 2024 between CPC members, Select Board members, Town Manager staff, Planning staff, Conservation staff, A.D. Makepeace representatives, consultants, attorneys, or lobbyists concerning the CPC item.

Search terms: "Makepeace", "A.D. Makepeace", "Redbrook", "Federal Furnace", "Garden Road", "cranberry", "CPC", and "Community Preservation". If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce all segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

title: 71 Hedges Pond Road / Cedarville Conservation Area disposition file, deeds, and mortgage records

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: 71 Hedges Pond Road / Cedarville Conservation Area disposition file, deeds, and mortgage records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request public records concerning the Town of Plymouth's disposition of 71 Hedges Pond Road / Cedarville Conservation Area to the Plymouth Regional Economic Development Foundation Inc. and the subsequent 2025 transaction involving Standish Investment Group LLC, Standish Investments LLC, E.J. Pontiff, or any Pontiff-affiliated buyer.

Please produce:

1. The Town-to-Foundation deed, purchase-and-sale agreement, land-disposition agreement, release, restriction, vote authorization, and closing file for the July 2022 transfer of 71 Hedges Pond Road / Cedarville Conservation Area from the Town of Plymouth to the Plymouth Regional Economic Development Foundation Inc.
2. Any Article 97, conservation restriction, conservation commission, town meeting, Select Board, Town Manager, or Town Counsel materials concerning whether the parcel was protected park, conservation, watershed, open-space, or Article 97 land.
3. Any records in Town custody identifying the later private buyer/grantee, including Standish Investment Group LLC, Standish Investments LLC, E.J. Pontiff, Cedar Meadow Cranberry, E.J. Pontiff Inc., or related entities.
4. Any recorded deed, mortgage, purchase-money mortgage, promissory note, closing statement, settlement statement, payment agreement, road-improvement agreement, or escrow document in Town custody concerning the February 7, 2025 Foundation-to-private-buyer transaction reported at approximately $3,450,000 and any approximately $2,000,000 road or State Road improvement payment back to the Town.
5. All communications from January 1, 2022 through present between the Town Manager, Select Board, Town Counsel, Plymouth Regional Economic Development Foundation Inc., Standish Investment Group LLC, Standish Investments LLC, E.J. Pontiff, Robb L. D'Ambruoso, or any Pontiff-affiliated entity concerning 71 Hedges Pond Road, Cedarville Conservation Area, State Road improvements, road-impact mitigation, or the 2025 sale.

title: FOIA: Shineful Energy (U.S. Department of State)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA: Shineful Energy (U.S. Department of State)
communications.communication:
May 19, 2026

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, as amended by the FOIA Improvement Act of 2016, and Treasury's implementing regulations at 31 C.F.R. Part 1, Subpart A, I request the records described in Part III below.

This request is focused. Public reporting based on internal PdVSA data indicates that since July 2025, Shineful Energy — a trading firm controlled by Ramón Carretero, a Panamanian businessperson the U.S. Treasury designated as a Specially Designated National in December 2025 for acting on behalf of the Government of Venezuela and for business dealings with the Maduro-Flores family — has won every contract to export PdVSA's share of crude from the Petroboscán field operated by Chevron, totaling roughly 11 million barrels and approximately $500 million in proceeds. The records sought are those necessary to understand (a) when and how OFAC identified Shineful Energy as a permitted or de facto offtaker for crude attributable to the Chevron-Venezuela license; (b) what due diligence, screening, or notice OFAC performed in connection with that arrangement; (c) what OFAC, the Office of Global Targeting, and Chevron knew about Carretero's relationship to Shineful Energy and to the Maduro-Flores family, and when; and (d) what enforcement action, if any, OFAC has taken in response.

Part I sets out matters already officially acknowledged so as to foreclose a Glomar response. Part II defines key terms. Part III sets out the records sought in priority order. Part IV addresses anticipated exemption claims. Parts V–VIII address search adequacy, format, fees and expedited processing, and procedure.

I. Matters already officially acknowledged
The following are matters of official public acknowledgment by the Department of the Treasury or other authoritative sources and accordingly cannot support a "Glomar" response or categorical Exemption 1 or Exemption 3 withholding under Wolf v. CIA, 473 F.3d 370 (D.C. Cir. 2007), and ACLU v. CIA, 710 F.3d 422 (D.C. Cir. 2013):

On or about July 24, 2025, OFAC issued a specific license authorizing Chevron Corporation to resume operations in Venezuela under conditions prohibiting cash payments to the Government of Venezuela, with payment to the Government of Venezuela to be made in oil rather than cash. The State Department publicly confirmed the existence of this arrangement on or about July 24, 2025.
In or about December 2025, OFAC designated Ramón Carretero as a Specially Designated National. Treasury's accompanying public statement asserted that Mr. Carretero "engaged in lucrative contracts with the Maduro regime and has had various business dealings with the Maduro-Flores family, including partnering in several companies together."

In December 2025, U.S. authorities seized a tanker publicly reported to be tied to Mr. Carretero.

On January 6, 2026, the President publicly stated that under a U.S.-Venezuela energy arrangement, the United States would market and sell Venezuelan oil and deposit proceeds into "U.S.-controlled" accounts, referencing "Sanctioned Oil" reportedly worth approximately $3 billion.

The Congressional Research Service has reported, on the basis of agency confirmation, that "[i]n July 2025, Treasury reportedly issued a specific license allowing Chevron's JVs to resume operations but prohibiting them from making cash payments to the Venezuelan government." CRS, Venezuela: Overview of U.S. Sanctions Policy, IF10715.
The existence of the specific license, the in-kind payment mechanism, Mr. Carretero's connections to the Maduro-Flores family, and Treasury's awareness of him are therefore matters of official acknowledgment.

II. Definitions
"Record" means any item within the meaning of 44 U.S.C. § 3301, including but not limited to final documents, drafts, marginalia, annotations, attachments, metadata, calendar entries, meeting notes (handwritten or electronic), briefing memoranda, talking points, decision memoranda, transmittal sheets, license applications, attestations, audit reports, vessel-tracking records, and visitor or access logs.

"Communication" means any record reflecting an exchange of information, in whatever medium, including email (on any domain), text/SMS, iMessage, Microsoft Teams, Slack, Signal, WhatsApp, voicemails, voicemail transcripts, telephone logs, and any messages on any ephemeral or encrypted messaging application used to conduct agency business. Consistent with the Federal Records Act and Competitive Enterprise Institute v. OSTP, 827 F.3d 145 (D.C. Cir. 2016), communications conducted on personal accounts or personal devices about official business are agency records and are within the scope of this request.

"Custodian" means any officer, employee, detailee, contractor, consultant, or other person acting on behalf of the agency, including any individual serving in a "special government employee" capacity or "acting" capacity, whose records may reasonably be calculated to contain responsive material.

"Shineful Entities" means Shineful Energy and any subsidiary, affiliate, predecessor, successor, alias, or front company associated with Shineful Energy or with Ramón Carretero, including any entity identified in OFAC's December 2025 designation materials.

"Carretero" means Ramón Carretero, a/k/a Ramon Carretero, Carretero Mendoza, and any other identified aliases.

Date range. Unless otherwise specified, January 1, 2025 through the date the agency conducts its search. Items concerning pre-existing OFAC knowledge of Mr. Carretero or the Shineful Entities are not bounded by start date.

III. Records requested (in priority order)
I ask that the agency produce records on a rolling basis in the priority order below.

Priority 1 — Pre-designation and pre-license knowledge of Carretero and Shineful
All records, from any date through July 31, 2025, in OFAC's possession (including OFAC's Office of Global Targeting, Licensing, Compliance and Enforcement, and Chief Counsel) referring or relating to Ramón Carretero, the Shineful Entities, or either's relationship to PdVSA, the Government of Venezuela, or the Maduro-Flores family. This Priority is intended to capture pre-existing intelligence, screening hits, prior license-application screening, prior enforcement referrals, and any analysis identifying Carretero or Shineful as risks before the issuance of the July 2025 Chevron specific license.

Priority 2 — The Carretero designation file
All records supporting OFAC's December 2025 designation of Mr. Carretero, including the action memorandum, evidentiary package, attribution analysis, redacted SDN listing rationale, any inter-agency concurrences, and any communications between OFAC and the Department of Justice, the Department of State, or the intelligence community concerning the designation.

Priority 3 — Identification of Shineful as offtaker
All records reflecting (a) when and how OFAC first became aware that the Shineful Entities had been awarded contracts to export PdVSA's share of crude from the Petroboscán field or any other Chevron-PdVSA joint venture; (b) any communications between OFAC and Chevron, between OFAC and PdVSA-adjacent persons, or among OFAC components concerning the identity of the offtaker(s) for PdVSA's share of the crude; and (c) any approval, no-action position, or interpretive guidance issued by OFAC concerning the offtaker arrangement.

Priority 4 — Chevron compliance submissions referencing Shineful
All compliance reports, attestations, KYC submissions, due-diligence files, beneficial-ownership disclosures, and other records submitted by or on behalf of Chevron Corporation pursuant to the July 2025 specific license (or any predecessor or successor license) that identify or reference the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, the Petroboscán offtake arrangement, or any trading firm awarded PdVSA's share of crude.

Priority 5 — OFAC due diligence on the offtaker arrangement
All internal OFAC due-diligence files, SDN-screening records, Worldcheck or commercial-database queries, beneficial-ownership analyses, and risk assessments concerning the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or the offtaker(s) selected for PdVSA's share of crude from the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures since July 1, 2025.

Priority 6 — Vessel, cargo, and shipping records
All records — including vessel-tracking data, shipping nominations, lifting schedules, bill-of-lading copies, AIS/EE records, and reports submitted by Chevron pursuant to license conditions — identifying any vessel or tanker that has loaded crude attributable to the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures since July 1, 2025, together with the identified consignee, end-buyer, and destination port. Records referencing tankers associated with the Shineful Entities or Mr. Carretero (including the tanker reportedly seized by U.S. authorities in December 2025) are specifically included.

Priority 7 — Records concerning destination and payment
Records sufficient to show (a) the destination jurisdictions for crude exported by the Shineful Entities under the offtake arrangements at issue (including but not limited to exports to the People's Republic of China); (b) the form and channels of payment for that crude, including any reports, intelligence, or analysis concerning the use of cryptocurrency or alternative payment mechanisms; and (c) the destination of proceeds.

Priority 8 — Enforcement records
All records concerning any enforcement action, investigation, license revocation, cease-and-desist, blocking notice, or referral to the Department of Justice related to the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or any vessel or transaction associated with either, including the December 2025 tanker seizure and any related forfeiture proceedings.

To the extent any portion of this Priority is withheld under Exemption 7(A), the agency is requested to identify the specific pending or prospective enforcement proceeding the disclosure would harm, consistent with NLRB v. Robbins Tire & Rubber Co., 437 U.S. 214 (1978).
Priority 9 — Communications between Treasury and Chevron concerning offtakers
All communications (as defined in Part II) between any custodian at Treasury — specifically including the Director of OFAC, OFAC Licensing, OFAC Office of Global Targeting, OFAC Chief Counsel, the Under Secretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence, and senior advisors to the Secretary — and any officer or employee of Chevron Corporation, any outside counsel or lobbyist representing Chevron, or any consultant acting on Chevron's behalf, concerning the identity, screening, approval, or compliance of the offtaker(s) for PdVSA's share of crude from the Chevron-PdVSA joint ventures, the Shineful Entities, Mr. Carretero, or the Petroboscán offtake arrangement.

Priority 10 — License terms concerning offtakers
A complete, unredacted copy of any provision of the July 2025 specific license (and any amendments, side letters, attestations, reporting conditions, or implementing instruments) addressing offtaker selection, offtaker screening, approval of trading counterparties, or restrictions on the identity of buyers, traders, or end-users of PdVSA's share of crude.

IV. Anticipated exemption claims and requester's position
Foreseeable harm. 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(8)(A)(i)(I) requires the agency to articulate a "reasonably foreseeable" harm for each withholding. Reporters Comm. for Freedom of the Press v. FBI, 3 F.4th 350 (D.C. Cir. 2021).

Segregability. 5 U.S.C. § 552(b) requires release of "[a]ny reasonably segregable portion of a record." Factual material is to be released even within otherwise deliberative documents, and a Vaughn index is requested for any record withheld in full.

Exemption 3 / IEEPA. Section 1702(c) of IEEPA (50 U.S.C. § 1702(c)) protects information submitted to the President in connection with IEEPA programs; it does not by its terms immunize the agency's own decisional documents, communications with a licensee, or screening records concerning a third-party offtaker.

Exemption 4. Information about Shineful Energy's status as offtaker, the Shineful-Carretero relationship, and OFAC's screening of either, is not information "obtained from" Chevron within the meaning of Food Marketing Institute v. Argus Leader Media, 588 U.S. 427 (2019), and the relevant facts have been substantially disclosed by Treasury itself in connection with the December 2025 Carretero designation. If any submitter notice is provided to Chevron under Executive Order 12600, the requester requests notice and an opportunity to be heard.

Exemption 5. Exemption 5 does not protect factual material; communications reflecting adopted reasoning or final agency position (NLRB v. Sears, 421 U.S. 132 (1975)); or material as to which any deliberative concerns have dissipated. The Carretero designation is a final agency action, and post-decisional materials concerning it are not deliberative.

Exemption 7(A). Categorical withholding is insufficient; the agency must identify a specific pending or prospective proceeding. NLRB v. Robbins Tire & Rubber Co., 437 U.S. 214 (1978).

Exemption 7(E). To the extent the agency invokes 7(E) for sanctions-screening "techniques and procedures," the requester notes that release of the results of screening as applied to Mr. Carretero and the Shineful Entities does not disclose the techniques themselves and is not within the exemption's scope.

V. Search adequacy
The search should be "reasonably calculated to uncover all relevant documents." Weisberg v. DOJ, 745 F.2d 1476 (D.C. Cir. 1984). It should include:

The .gov email accounts of the custodians, plus any personal email accounts or personal devices used by those custodians to conduct agency business during the relevant period;
Text/SMS, iMessage, Signal, WhatsApp, Microsoft Teams, and any other messaging platform used by those custodians for agency business;
OFAC's internal databases including the SDN List management system, OFAC's licensing case-management system, and OFAC's Office of Global Targeting working files;
Calendar entries, voicemails, hard-copy records, and records held by contractors, detailees, or special government employees with relevant portfolios.
If the agency claims that any portion returns "no responsive records," the requester requests a declaration describing the search methodology, the systems and accounts searched, the search terms applied (including but not limited to "Shineful," "Carretero," "Carretero Mendoza," "Petroboscán," "Petroboscan," "offtaker," "offtake," "lifter," "lifting," and "Maduro-Flores"), and the custodians queried.

The agency is requested to take immediate steps to preserve all potentially responsive records, including ephemeral and auto-deleting messages, pending completion of this request.

VI. Format of production
Production in native format with metadata intact (.msg / .eml for email; .pdf only where originally PDF; native Office formats for native Office documents). For text messages and chat records, production in a structured export preserving sender, recipient, timestamp, and thread. Rolling production beginning with Priority 1.

For redacted records, please cite the specific exemption next to each redaction and provide a Vaughn index for any record withheld in full.

VII. Fee waiver and expedited processing
Fee waiver. Pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) and 31 C.F.R. § 1.7. The records sought go directly to whether Treasury's licensing scheme has, in operation, channeled hundreds of millions of dollars in oil proceeds to a person Treasury itself has now designated as a Specially Designated National for ties to the Maduro-Flores family. Disclosure is "likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government" and is not primarily in my commercial interest. I [will publish / will share with affiliated journalists / am affiliated with the news media as defined in 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II)]. [Insert two to three sentences specifying your dissemination capacity.]

In the alternative, please treat me as a "representative of the news media" for fee purposes. Please contact me before assessing fees in excess of $25, and please provide an itemized estimate rather than a categorical assertion of burden.

Expedited processing. Pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E) and 31 C.F.R. § 1.5(e). There is "[a]n urgency to inform the public concerning actual or alleged Federal Government activity" because (a) the offtake arrangement remains operative; (b) Treasury's own December 2025 designation establishes that the principal beneficiary of the arrangement is a person sanctioned for ties to the Maduro regime; and (c) congressional and judicial deliberation on Venezuela policy is ongoing and will be informed by the records sought.

I certify under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E)(vi) that the foregoing statements made in support of expedited processing are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

VIII. Procedural matters
I ask that the agency:

Acknowledge receipt within ten working days and assign a tracking number.
Inform me immediately if any portion is being referred to another agency or component.
Confer with me before deeming any portion improperly described or unduly burdensome.
Treat the ten Priorities above as severable, so that production of Priority 1 records is not delayed by the agency's processing of subsequent Priorities.
Make a determination on expedited processing within the 10-calendar-day statutory window under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(E)(ii)(I).
If the agency has not made a substantive determination within 20 working days, I intend to treat the lapse as a constructive denial and pursue all available administrative and judicial remedies, including appeal to the Treasury FOIA Appeals Officer and, where appropriate, suit under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(B).

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,

Jessica M Kravitz

title: Obama Presidential Library: NSC Records on U.S. Public Messaging re: 2014 Crimea Annexation

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: NSC Records on U.S. Public Messaging re: 2014 Crimea Annexation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request access to unclassified records held by the Barack Obama Presidential Library falling within the date range of February 20, 2014 – April 30, 2014, relating to the U.S. government's public messaging strategy regarding Russia's annexation of Crimea.

Category A — Talking Points and Press Guidance
Daily press guidance, talking points, or messaging memoranda prepared for or distributed to White House and/or Department of State public affairs teams concerning: the situation in Crimea; the presence of unmarked Russian forces ("little green men" / "polite people" / Russian: "вежливые люди"); the March 2014 referendum; Ukrainian sovereignty; the Budapest Memorandum; or Russian justifications based on "protection of compatriots" or "Russian-speakers."

Category B — NSC Strategic Communications Records
Internal strategy memoranda or emails originating from or addressed to the NSC Office of the Deputy National Security Advisor for Strategic Communications discussing the administration's messaging response to Russian narratives regarding Crimea.

Category C — Named Official Correspondence
Records created or received by:

Ben Rhodes, Deputy National Security Advisor for Strategic Communications (likely email address of: Rhodes@nss.eop.gov and/or Rhodes@nsc.eop.gov and/or brhodes@nsc.eop.gov)
Celeste Wallander, Senior Director for Russia/Eurasia (likely email address of: celeste.wallander@who.eop.gov or Wallander@who.eop.gov or cwallander@who.eop.gov)


II. Scope and Search Guidance

Please prioritize the following record series: National Security Council: Records: Subject Files and Country Files (Russia/Ukraine).

Suggested search terms: Crimea, вежливые люди, annexation, little green men, polite people, referendum, Budapest Memorandum, sovereignty, protection of compatriots, Russian-speakers.

Excluded from this request: scheduling records, travel logistics, media credentials, and personal administrative correspondence.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: Idaho Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — CMGC2024-105 Complete Procurement File
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Idaho Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The complete procurement file for solicitation CMGC2024-105,
Recycled Water Program Improvements Project (RWP 010), including:

1. NAMES AND AFFILIATIONS: The names, titles, departmental affiliations,
   and qualifications of all evaluation committee members who reviewed
   and scored responses to this solicitation.

2. INDIVIDUAL SCORE SHEETS: The individual evaluator score sheets
   showing the scores assigned by each committee member to each
   proposing firm, including any written comments, notes, or
   justifications recorded by evaluators during scoring.

3. FINAL SCORES AND RANKING: The final consensus score sheet and
   complete ranking of all firms that submitted proposals.

4. INTENT TO AWARD: The Intent to Award letter and all documentation
   supporting the award recommendation, including the stated contract
   value, scope of services, and any conditions or contingencies.

5. COUNCIL APPROVAL DETERMINATION: Any written determination, analysis,
   memorandum, or correspondence addressing whether this award required
   Boise City Council approval, including any dollar threshold, statutory
   provision, or delegation authority cited as the basis for the award
   authority used.

6. BID PROTEST RECORDS: Any bid protests, objections, or challenges
   received from proposing firms, and the city's responses or
   determinations regarding each.

7. EVALUATION COMMITTEE CORRESPONDENCE: All correspondence (including
   emails, memoranda, meeting notes, and written instructions) between
   the Purchasing division and the evaluation committee members regarding
   this solicitation, including any direction provided to evaluators
   about scoring methodology, evaluation criteria weighting, or
   treatment of specific proposers.

8. SIGNED CONTRACT: The final signed contract or agreement between the
   City of Boise and the awarded firm, including:
   a. The complete contract document with all exhibits, appendices,
      and attachments
   b. The pre-construction services agreement or scope
   c. The payment schedule, fee structure, and any guaranteed maximum
      price provisions
   d. All amendments, change orders, or modifications executed to date
   e. The stated total contract value or not-to-exceed amount

9. CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURES: Any conflict-of-interest disclosure
   forms, ethics certifications, or recusal statements signed by or
   pertaining to each evaluation committee member in connection with
   this solicitation.

10. SOLICITATION REVIEW FORM: The solicitation review form, procurement
    checklist, or equivalent internal document used to document and
    verify that each step of the procurement process was completed in
    accordance with city policy.

11. OFFEROR LIST: The list of all firms that attended any pre-proposal
    conference, submitted questions during the solicitation period,
    or requested solicitation documents.

12. SHORT LIST DOCUMENTATION: Any documentation of a short list
    determination, including the criteria used to select firms for
    interviews and the firms selected.

Date range for correspondence (Items 5, 7): January 1, 2024 through
December 31, 2024.

preferred; native format such as Excel for score sheets if available).

required by Idaho Code § 74-113.

title: FBI: FBI file 100-SF-25972 - Victor Vasilevich Afanassiev - Афанасьев Владимир Викторович

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI: FBI file 100-SF-25972 - Victor Vasilevich Afanassiev - Афанасьев Владимир Викторович
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the search, review, and disclosure of all records, correspondence, cross-references, investigative reports, and "Director's brief" cables contained within or associated with the following specific FBI file:

File Number: 100-SF-25972

This file is understood to be a Hoover-era Classification 100 (Domestic Security) investigation maintained by the San Francisco Field Office, dating approximately from the late 1940s to early 1950s, which likely generated direct high-level correspondence to FBI Director J. Edgar Hoover and agent Edward Tamm. 

Because historical records from this era frequently suffer from phonetic transliteration variances by field agents, I request that this file search incorporate and cross-reference the following name variations to ensure a complete file retrieval:

Victor Vasilevich Afanassiev (and alternative spellings: Afanasiev, Afanas'ev)

Vladimir Viktorovich Afanasiev (Cyrillic: Афанасьев Владимир Викторович)

II. Historical Context & Subject Identity Clarification

A partial reasoning for this request is to definitively identify whether the individual targeted in file 100-SF-25972 is:

A Soviet diplomat, cultural attache, or cultural figure (such as a visiting actor/delegate) operating in the United States/San Francisco region during the post-WWII era; OR

The early records of the Soviet political dissident and national later incarcerated in the USSR's Vladimir Prison network following political/escape convictions in 1949 and 1972.

III. Privacy Act and Deceased Status (The 100-Year / Historical Rule)

As these records originate from an active investigation initiated over 75 years ago (circa 1940s), the subjects of this file are either deceased or their birth dates fall well within the FBI’s "100-Year Rule" regarding foreign nationals and historical figures. To the extent that third-party privacy considerations arise, the historical public interest in understanding Hoover-era Domestic Security tracking of Soviet nationals heavily outweighs any residual privacy interests under Exemptions (b)(6) and (b)(7)(C).

According to a blog post read, it is important that I state the following: "As you know, the Federal Bureau of Investigation is required to search the records stored at NARA’s Washington Records Center in Suitland, MD, but still technically under the FBI’s control. For more information see 36 CFR 1250.8" Please also conduct a physical search of records at NARA's Washington Records Center in Suitland, for any records or documents related to File Number: 100-SF-25972 aka "Victor Vasilevich Afanassiev".

If the bureau cannot fulfill certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you to the bureau for the commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

District of Columbia

Jeremiah Regan 441 4th Street NW Washington, DC 20001 jeremiah.regan@dc.gov Phone: (202) 727-0252 Please know that, under D.C. Official Code § 2-537 and 1 DCMR 412, you have the right to appeal this response to the Mayor of the District of Columbia.  If you elect to request an administrative review of this request response by the Mayor, your appeal must be in writing and contain “Freedom of Information Act Appeal” or “FOIA Appeal” in the subject line of the letter as well as on the outside of the envelope.  The appeal must include (1) a copy of the original request; (2) a copy of any written denial; (3) a statement of the circumstances, reasons, and/or arguments advanced in support of disclosure; and (4) a daytime telephone number, and e-mail and/or U.S. Mail address at which you can be reached. Electronic versions of the same information can also be e-mailed to foia.appeals@dc.gov.  Further, a copy of all appeal materials must be forward to the Freedom of Information Officer of the involved agency or to the agency head of that agency if there is no designated Freedom of Information Officer there. title: ILJAOC via Brea PD (CA) - PSDEX/AVCC Contributions and Data Sharing

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: ILJAOC via Brea PD (CA) - PSDEX/AVCC Contributions and Data Sharing
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Foundational Documents: The Accurint Virtual Crime Center 'AVCC XML Addendum' or 'PSDEX Addendum' executed by ILJAOC, including all sub-agency addendums executed by member agencies.

2) Who Contributes: Any documentation, configurations, or records that indicate whether ILJAOC members contribute data to PSDEX/AVCC (Public Safety Data Exchange / Accurint Virtual Crime Center).

3) Who Can Access Agency Data: Any documentation, configurations, or records that indicate what agencies may access contributed data. Specifically, we seek to understand whether ILJAOC members who contribute data to PSDEX/AVCC restrict data access to only ILJAOC members, or if agency data flows into the national PSDEX database available to other AVCC customers.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All records identifying government entities, agencies, municipalities, or authorized purchasers that have purchased goods or services through WA State Master Contract No. 00220 (LexisNexis Risk Solutions FL Inc., Category 3: Nationwide Public Records Search), including any Master Contract Sales Reports submitted by LexisNexis Risk Solutions FL Inc. to Enterprise Services.

2. Any purchase orders, participating addenda, or order forms issued under Master Contract No. 00220 by law enforcement agencies, sheriff's offices, police departments, or criminal justice agencies, for the period July 1, 2022 through the date this request is processed.

Context: We are looking for Washington State agencies that are contributing data to LexisNexis's PSDEX (public safety data exchange) or AVCC (Accurint virtual crime center).

communications.communication:
DES PRR 26-0368,

This is an acknowledgment of your public records request below. On this website Contract Summary<https://apps.des.wa.gov/DESContracts/Home/ContractSummary/00220> you will find pricing exhibits, and contracts/amendments for Lexis Nexis. You can find this information as you scroll down under “Awarded Vendors” or a little bit further down there is a drop-down menu titled “Current Documents” that has these records as well. And on this website Statewide Contract (Master Contract) Sales Data by Customer, Contract, Vendor | Data.WA | State of Washington<https://data.wa.gov/Procurements-and-Contracts/Statewide-Contract-Master-Contract-Sales-Data-by-C/n8q6-4twj/data_preview>, you can find purchase and sales data. In the top right corner, there is a search bar where you can search for “00220” or “Employment income” and see the sales reported data and who purchased for this contract. There is a screen shot below showing this search as an example. It is important to use the “” symbols when typing in the contract number in the search to get an accurate search.

DES would not have purchase orders, participating addenda, or order forms as those would come from the departments that have used the contract themselves.

With this response your request is now closed, and this is our final response. DES does not intend to further address your public records request. The PRA’s one year statue of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of date of this communication. DES encourages you to ask any follow-up questions regarding our response. DES encourages you to notify DES of any concerns you may have about the adequacy of our response, compliance with the PRA or general concerns about any records we have provided. Please ask any question on or before May 26, 2026.

title: Port of Seattle Police Department Dive Unit Records (2010-2011) / Reference: Detective Steven Ivey Underwater Ordnance Recovery

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Port of Seattle Police Department Dive Unit Records (2010-2011) / Reference: Detective Steven Ivey Underwater Ordnance Recovery
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to and copies of public records regarding the Port of Seattle Police Department (POSPD) Dive Team's discovery, location, and recovery operations of "live" and "spent" World War II and Vietnam War era munitions/shells at the cruise ship terminals (Terminal 91 / Smith Cove).

To assist in locating these records, please note that these operations occurred primarily between April 2010 and December 2011, and heavily involved Detective Steven Ivey of the POSPD Dive Team (as publicly cited in his 2012 Officer of the Year commendation).

For context:
https://maritime-executive.com/article/video-additional-munitions-discovered-under-seattle-cruise-ship-terminal

A publicly accessible document details Officer Ivey's involvement:
https://scctv-portals-pos.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/attachments/2012/1298/RM_20120626_4b.pdf

Specifically, I am requesting the following items within the timeframe of April 1, 2010, through December 31, 2011:

Video Footage: Any and all video recordings, including but not limited to underwater SCUBA dive video footage, above-water operational or briefing videos, and media/surveillance or drone footage captures related to these specific underwater ordnance sweeps and multi-agency EOD recovery actions.

Operational & Investigative Reports: Any dive logs, incident reports, after-action reports, or tactical summaries detailing Detective Steven Ivey’s (and the broader dive team's) multi-hour underwater recovery efforts at the cruise terminals.

Photographs: Any underwater or topside photographs taken by POSPD personnel documenting the discovered munitions, the dive operations, or the handoff to military EOD units.

If any portion of these records is deemed exempt from disclosure, please redact the exempt portions and provide the remaining non-exempt records, along with an itemized exemption log citing the specific statutory exemption under RCW 42.56.

Thank you for your time and assistance with this request.

Suggested Search & Indexing Terms:
To assist with internal database, email, and archiving searches, the Port of Seattle may have cataloged these records under any of the following terms, which are included in the scope of this request:

Personnel/Units: "Steven Ivey", "Det. Ivey", "Detective Ivey", "POSPD Dive", "Dive Team", "Explosive Ordnance Disposal", "EOD".

Locations: "Terminal 91", "T-91", "Pier 90", "Pier 91", "Smith Cove", "Magnolia Bridge".

Items/Operations: "Munitions", "Ordnance", "WWII shells", "Vietnam shells", "Underwater sweep", "DMM", "UXO" (Unexploded Ordnance), "Officer of the Year".

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Freedom of Information Act Request BWC & Dash Camera Footage Kyle Hennessey

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Freedom of Information Act Request BWC & Dash Camera Footage Kyle Hennessey
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Freedom of Information Act Request — Body Camera & Dash Camera Footage
Case Number: SLCPD-21-030652

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act and applicable state public records laws, I request copies of the following records:

INCIDENT DETAILS
  Date/Time:  2021-03-06 at 14:15
  Location:   St. Louis County, Missouri (Florissant area)
  Suspect:    Kyle J. Hennessey
  Charges:    Failure to Stop for Law Enforcement — Felony (RSMo 575.150), Assault of a Law Enforcement Officer (Second Degree), Operating a Motor Vehicle Without a Valid License, Property Damage (struck patrol vehicle)
  Case #:     SLCPD-21-030652

RECORDS REQUESTED

1. ALL body-worn camera (BWC) footage from each of the following officers and any additional officers who responded to or were present during this incident:

  • Deputy Aaron Lindqvist Badge #ST-0892 (Pursuing officer)
  • Deputy Renee Okafor Badge #ST-1044 (Secondary unit, spike strip deployment)

2. ALL dash camera / in-car video footage from every patrol vehicle, marked or unmarked, involved in the pursuit or apprehension.

3. Any supplemental video including aerial/overhead, pole-mounted cameras, and surveillance footage retained as part of the investigation.

4. Audio dispatch recordings and radio communications from the initial call through the conclusion of the incident.

FORMAT: Please provide records in original digital format (MP4 for video, WAV/MP3 for audio). Electronic delivery via secure download preferred.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brandon Martin

title: FOIA Request: Policy 430 - Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Policy 430 - Automated License Plate Readers (ALPR)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

According to Forest Park Police Department Policy 430, with reference to 625 ILCS 5/2-130, it is stated

- Section III / F: "The Chief of Police or their designee, serves as the ALPR Administrator for the agency"
- Section IV Procedures:
A: "No member of this department shall operate ALPR equipment or access ALPR data without first completing department-approved training"
D: "ALPR Administrator shall monitor to ensure the security of the information and compliance with applicable privacy laws"
G: "If an ALPR is used...a Street Camera / LPR Use Form must be completed and forwarded to the Patrol Lieutenant who will keep a record of all uses"
- Section I Investigative Procedures
Q1: "ALPR system audits should be conducted on a regular basis"
Q2: "The Chief or their designee should download and save monthly ALPR system User and Access data for at least two years and one month"

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records

The documents I am seeking include, but are not limited to:
1) Any document recording the name of the Forest Park ALPR Administrator, if this role is not fulfilled by the Chief of Police.

2) Training Materials: The department-approved training materials used as a prerequisite for sworn officers to use or access ALPR data as per section IV - A

3) Training Records: Certificates of completion or other records showing sworn officer completion of the training provided to staff as per section IV - A

4) Policies, procedures and records of how the ALPR Administrator monitors to ensure the security of the ALPR information to ensure compliance with applicable privacy laws as per section IV - D

5) Copies of the Street Camera / LPR Use Forms from September 2025 to present as per section IV - G

6) Policies, procedures and records of the ALPR system audits conducted per section Q of Policy 430

7) Monthly ALPR system User and Access data for the last two years and one month as per section Q


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

ll
